verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is a canonical ER-resident intracellular calcium channel. Its architecture (large N-terminal cytoplasmic ligand-binding domain, juxtamembrane domain, and C-terminal TM domain) is embedded in the ER membrane, with no domain facing the extracellular space. Immunolocalization consistently places it in the ER (and sub-apical ER in polarized epithelial cells). Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet display under stress, cancer state, apoptosis, or activation; overexpressed ITPR3 mislocalizes to MAMs/nucleus, both intracellular; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no germline/developmental outer-leaflet display reported; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — ITPR3 is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — 'sub-apical ER near the plasma membrane' is not a PM pool; no surface biotinylation or flow cytometry evidence of PM residence; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no covalent or wash-resistant tethering to a TM partner on the plasma membrane. No cell-surface-directed clinical program found. Verdict: endomembrane-resident.
